original_reasoning: الشِّياص = شراسة الخلق (ferocity of character); مشايصة = منافرة (hostility, mutual aversion). ἅλωσις = capture, conquest. Ferocity and hostility lead naturally to conflict, and conflict leads to conquest. The chain ferocity→aggression→conquest is a genuine causal-semantic pathway.
Plausible crafted-plant overlap: Arabic is an object made from fresh reeds/branches; Greek means made from gourds.

الخشب: ما غلظ من العيدان (thick/coarse wood/timber). λέχος=bed, couch. Beds are made of wood. This is a clear material-to-product relationship: خشب (timber) → λέχος (bed/wooden furniture). Direct manufacturing chain.
Plausible succession link: Arabic includes coming from behind, Greek means descendant or child of Seleucus.

Arabic ja'falieq = bishop or chief of Christians (al-jathaliq, catholicos). Greek enkephalos = brain (en+kephale 'in the head'). Bishop-as-head-of-church and brain-in-head share the 'head/governing center' core. Plausible deep link, especially since 'jathaliq' may itself be Greek-derived.

Arabic khaza'a = to take away, cut off, separate. Greek schiza = a piece of wood cut off, splinter, lath. Cutting off action and the cut-off piece are direct cause-result. Phonetic resemblance khz/schz is notable. Strong link.
نجخ=to make lustful/throat noise; κυνάγχη=tonsilitis/throat disease. Possible semantic link via throat affliction
Plausible object-core link: Arabic includes gathering, Greek is a case that gathers/stores valuables.
